title: Argentinian Fermented Crisp Terrine With Tomato Emulsion
description: A halal-friendly, emulsified, fermented Argentinian dish featuring a crisp, tangy crust and a rich, savory interior. This recipe makes 6 servings and can be served as a snack or as a side dish for a romantic dinner.

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup grated onion
ingredients.notes: add 1/4 cup more if you prefer a stronger onion flavor

ingredients.name: 2 cloves garlic
ingredients.notes: optional, omit for a non-garlicky flavor

ingredients.name: 1 tsp paprika
ingredients.notes: optional, omit for a non-smoked flavor

ingredients.name: 1 tsp salt
ingredients.notes: adjust to taste, omit for low-sodium diet

ingredients.name: 1 cup vegemite or yeast extract
ingredients.notes: optional, omit for a non-yeast flavor

ingredients.name: 1 cup grated cheddar cheese
ingredients.notes: optional, omit for a non-dairy diet

instructions: In a blender or food processor, blend the bread, tomato juice, onion, garlic, paprika, salt, and black pepper until the mixture is smooth.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and stir in the parsley, vegemite, and cheddar cheese (if using).
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to allow the flavors to meld.
After 2 hours, remove the dish from the refrigerator and use a spatula to scrape the crust from the sides and bottom of the dish.
Transfer the crust to a baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
Once the crust is crispy, remove it from the oven and let it cool completely.
To serve, cut the cooled crust into 6 equal pieces and serve with the chilled terrine (the mixture in the baking dish).
Garnish with additional parsley, if desired.
